text:
Market today: Mood is cautious-to-bearish. The real driver is a growing narrative that the chip/AI bull run is cracking — traders are hedging, not buying. That hits your concentrated tech/chip book directly, and margin makes every down-move sting harder.

Your holdings: PLTR (-12.74%) is flagged SELL — it's underwater and the scanner wants it gone before it gets worse. AVGO (-12.89%) is your largest position and flagged TRIM — headline about the OpenAI Jalapeno chip is a potential catalyst but it needs to deliver. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec already deep in the red with a stop at $9.50 — tiny positions can gap through stops fast. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited data — treat it as a live grenade on margin.

Watch: 1) Any AVGO-specific reaction to the Jalapeno/OpenAI chip news — that's your largest position and it needs a win. 2) Broad chip sentiment — if SMH (your only green chip name) starts rolling over, that's your early warning.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers are also your biggest risks on margin — PLTR and AVGO need decisions today, not tomorrow.
